output: The first class interferes with the subject-verb agreement by changing the conjugated form of the main verb or auxiliary verb. The second class similarly modifies the declined form of the adjective and therefore violates the agreement in case, number, and gender. For the third class, we manipulate the syntax by deleting 2-3 random words from the sentence, depending on the length. The last class impacts the orthography, which is achieved by using data provided by Spellchecker.lu, a semiautomatic spellchecking website frequently used in Luxembourg. We changed one random word in the sentence by using the least frequent variant in the spellchecker data.
